110:   end)

136:   # Returns a SQL conditions string used to find all issues visible by the specified user
137:   def self.visible_condition(user, options={})
138:     Project.allowed_to_condition(user, :view_issues, options) do |role, user|
139:       sql =
140:         if user.id && user.logged?
141:           case role.issues_visibility
142:           when 'all'
143:             '1=1'
144:           when 'default'
145:             user_ids = [user.id] + user.groups.pluck(:id).compact
146:             "(#{table_name}.is_private = #{connection.quoted_false} " \
147:               "OR #{table_name}.author_id = #{user.id} " \
148:               "OR #{table_name}.assigned_to_id IN (#{user_ids.join(',')}))"
149:           when 'own'
150:             user_ids = [user.id] + user.groups.pluck(:id).compact
151:             "(#{table_name}.author_id = #{user.id} OR " \
152:               "#{table_name}.assigned_to_id IN (#{user_ids.join(',')}))"
153:           else
154:             '1=0'
155:           end
156:         else
157:           "(#{table_name}.is_private = #{connection.quoted_false})"
158:         end
159:       unless role.permissions_all_trackers?(:view_issues)
160:         tracker_ids = role.permissions_tracker_ids(:view_issues)
161:         if tracker_ids.any?
162:           sql = "(#{sql} AND #{table_name}.tracker_id IN (#{tracker_ids.join(',')}))"
163:         else
164:           sql = '1=0'
165:         end
166:       end
167:       sql
168:     end
169:   end

171:   # Returns true if usr or current user is allowed to view the issue
172:   def visible?(usr=nil)
173:     (usr || User.current).allowed_to?(:view_issues, self.project) do |role, user|
174:       visible =
175:         if user.logged?
176:           case role.issues_visibility
177:           when 'all'
178:             true
179:           when 'default'
180:             !self.is_private? || (self.author == user || user.is_or_belongs_to?(assigned_to))
181:           when 'own'
182:             self.author == user || user.is_or_belongs_to?(assigned_to)
183:           else
184:             false
185:           end
186:         else
187:           !self.is_private?
188:         end
189:       unless role.permissions_all_trackers?(:view_issues)
190:         visible &&= role.permissions_tracker_ids?(:view_issues, tracker_id)
191:       end
192:       visible
193:     end
194:   end

199:   end

237:   def initialize(attributes=nil, *args)
238:     super

247:     super()

253:   # AR#Persistence#destroy would raise and RecordNotFound exception
254:   # if the issue was already deleted or updated (non matching lock_version).
255:   # This is a problem when bulk deleting issues or deleting a project
256:   # (because an issue may already be deleted if its parent was deleted
257:   # first).
258:   # The issue is reloaded by the nested_set before being deleted so
259:   # the lock_version condition should not be an issue but we handle it.
260:   def destroy
261:     super
262:   rescue ActiveRecord::StaleObjectError, ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound
263:     # Stale or already deleted
264:     begin
265:       reload
266:     rescue ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound
267:       # The issue was actually already deleted
268:       @destroyed = true
269:       return freeze
270:     end
271:     # The issue was stale, retry to destroy
272:     super
273:   end

277:     @workflow_rule_by_attribute = nil
278:     @assignable_versions = nil
279:     @relations = nil
280:     @spent_hours = nil
281:     @total_spent_hours = nil
282:     @total_estimated_hours = nil
283:     @last_updated_by = nil
284:     @last_notes = nil
285:     base_reload(*)
286:   end

305:   # Copies attributes from another issue, arg can be an id or an Issue
306:   def copy_from(arg, options={})
307:     issue = arg.is_a?(Issue) ? arg : Issue.visible.find(arg)
308:     self.attributes =
309:       issue.attributes.dup.except(
310:         "id", "root_id", "parent_id", "lft", "rgt",
311:         "created_on", "updated_on", "status_id", "closed_on"
312:       )
313:     self.custom_field_values =
314:       issue.custom_field_values.to_h do |v|
315:         [v.custom_field_id, v.value]
316:       end
317:     if options[:keep_status]
318:       self.status = issue.status
319:     end
320:     self.author = User.current
321:     unless options[:attachments] == false
322:       self.attachments = issue.attachments.map do |attachement|
323:         attachement.copy(:container => self)
324:       end
325:     end
326: 
327:     unless options[:watchers] == false
328:       self.watcher_user_ids = issue.visible_watcher_users.select{|u| u.status == User::STATUS_ACTIVE}.map(&:id)
329:     end
330:     @copied_from = issue
331:     @copy_options = options
332:     self
333:   end

335:   # Returns an unsaved copy of the issue
336:   def copy(attributes=nil, copy_options={})
337:     copy = self.class.new.copy_from(self, copy_options)
338:     copy.attributes = attributes if attributes
339:     copy
340:   end

378:     if tracker_id.to_s != self.tracker_id.to_s
379:       self.tracker = (tracker_id.present? ? Tracker.find_by_id(tracker_id) : nil)

384:   # Sets the tracker.
385:   # This will set the status to the default status of the new tracker if:
386:   # * the status was the default for the previous tracker
387:   # * or if the status was not part of the new tracker statuses
388:   # * or the status was nil
389:   def tracker=(tracker)
390:     tracker_was = self.tracker
391:     association(:tracker).writer(tracker)
392:     if tracker != tracker_was
393:       if status == tracker_was.try(:default_status)
394:         self.status = nil
395:       elsif status && tracker && !tracker.issue_status_ids.include?(status.id)
396:         self.status = nil
397:       end
398:       reassign_custom_field_values
399:       @workflow_rule_by_attribute = nil
400:     end
401:     self.status ||= default_status
402:     self.tracker
403:   end

405:   def project_id=(project_id)
406:     if project_id.to_s != self.project_id.to_s
407:       self.project = (project_id.present? ? Project.find_by_id(project_id) : nil)
408:     end
409:     self.project_id

412:   # Sets the project.
413:   # Unless keep_tracker argument is set to true, this will change the tracker
414:   # to the first tracker of the new project if the previous tracker is not part
415:   # of the new project trackers.
416:   # This will:
417:   # * clear the fixed_version is it's no longer valid for the new project.
418:   # * clear the parent issue if it's no longer valid for the new project.
419:   # * set the category to the category with the same name in the new
420:   #   project if it exists, or clear it if it doesn't.
421:   # * for new issue, set the fixed_version to the project default version
422:   #   if it's a valid fixed_version.
423:   def project=(project, keep_tracker=false)
424:     project_was = self.project
425:     association(:project).writer(project)
426:     if project != project_was
427:       @safe_attribute_names = nil
428:     end
429:     if project_was && project && project_was != project
430:       @assignable_versions = nil
431: 
432:       unless keep_tracker || project.trackers.include?(tracker)
433:         self.tracker = project.trackers.first
434:       end
435:       # Reassign to the category with same name if any
436:       if category
437:         self.category = project.issue_categories.find_by_name(category.name)
438:       end
439:       # Clear the assignee if not available in the new project for new issues (eg. copy)
440:       # For existing issue, the previous assignee is still valid, so we keep it
441:       if new_record? && assigned_to && !assignable_users.include?(assigned_to)
442:         self.assigned_to_id = nil
443:       end
444:       # Keep the fixed_version if it's still valid in the new_project
445:       if fixed_version && fixed_version.project != project && !project.shared_versions.include?(fixed_version)
446:         self.fixed_version = nil
447:       end
448:       # Clear the parent task if it's no longer valid
449:       unless valid_parent_project?
450:         self.parent_issue_id = nil
451:       end
452:       reassign_custom_field_values
453:       @workflow_rule_by_attribute = nil
454:     end
455:     # Set fixed_version to the project default version if it's valid
456:     if new_record? && fixed_version.nil? && project && project.default_version_id?
457:       if project.shared_versions.open.exists?(project.default_version_id)
458:         self.fixed_version_id = project.default_version_id
459:       end
460:     end
461:     self.project
462:   end

541:   def safe_attribute_names(user=nil)
542:     names = super
543:     names -= disabled_core_fields
544:     names -= read_only_attribute_names(user)
545:     if new_record?
546:       # Make sure that project_id can always be set for new issues
547:       names |= %w(project_id)
548:     end
549:     if dates_derived?
550:       names -= %w(start_date due_date)
551:     end
552:     if priority_derived?
553:       names -= %w(priority_id)
554:     end
555:     if done_ratio_derived?
556:       names -= %w(done_ratio)
557:     end
558:     names
559:   end

561:   # Safely sets attributes
562:   # Should be called from controllers instead of #attributes=
563:   # attr_accessible is too rough because we still want things like
564:   # Issue.new(:project => foo) to work
565:   def safe_attributes=(attrs, user=User.current)
566:     if attrs.respond_to?(:to_unsafe_hash)
567:       attrs = attrs.to_unsafe_hash
568:     end
569: 
570:     @attributes_set_by = user
571:     return unless attrs.is_a?(Hash)
572: 
573:     attrs = attrs.deep_dup
574: 
575:     # Project and Tracker must be set before since new_statuses_allowed_to depends on it.
576:     if (p = attrs.delete('project_id')) && safe_attribute?('project_id')
577:       if p.is_a?(String) && !/^\d*$/.match?(p)
578:         p_id = Project.find_by_identifier(p).try(:id)
579:       else
580:         p_id = p.to_i
581:       end
582:       if allowed_target_projects(user).where(:id => p_id).exists?
583:         self.project_id = p_id
584:       end
585: 
586:       if project_id_changed? && attrs['category_id'].present? && attrs['category_id'].to_s == category_id_was.to_s
587:         # Discard submitted category on previous project
588:         attrs.delete('category_id')
589:       end
590:     end
591: 
592:     if (t = attrs.delete('tracker_id')) && safe_attribute?('tracker_id')
593:       if allowed_target_trackers(user).where(:id => t.to_i).exists?
594:         self.tracker_id = t
595:       end
596:     end
597:     if project && tracker.nil?
598:       # Set a default tracker to accept custom field values
599:       # even if tracker is not specified
600:       allowed_trackers = allowed_target_trackers(user)
601: 
602:       if attrs['parent_issue_id'].present?
603:         # If parent_issue_id is present, the first tracker for which this field
604:         # is not disabled is chosen as default
605:         self.tracker = allowed_trackers.detect {|t| t.core_fields.include?('parent_issue_id')}
606:       end
607:       self.tracker ||= allowed_trackers.first
608:     end
609: 
610:     statuses_allowed = new_statuses_allowed_to(user)
611:     if (s = attrs.delete('status_id')) && safe_attribute?('status_id')
612:       if statuses_allowed.collect(&:id).include?(s.to_i)
613:         self.status_id = s
614:       end
615:     end
616:     if new_record? && !statuses_allowed.include?(status)
617:       self.status = statuses_allowed.first || default_status
618:     end
619:     # Use the selected tracker's private default when the form has no explicit value.
620:     if new_record? && tracker&.private_by_default? &&
621:          !attrs.key?('is_private') && safe_attribute?('is_private', user)
622:       attrs['is_private'] = '1'
623:     end
624:     if (u = attrs.delete('assigned_to_id')) && safe_attribute?('assigned_to_id')
625:       self.assigned_to_id = u
626:     end
627:     attrs = delete_unsafe_attributes(attrs, user)
628:     return if attrs.empty?
629: 
630:     if attrs['parent_issue_id'].present?
631:       s = attrs['parent_issue_id'].to_s
632:       unless (m = s.match(%r{\A#?(\d+)\z})) && (m[1] == parent_id.to_s || Issue.visible(user).exists?(m[1]))
633:         @invalid_parent_issue_id = attrs.delete('parent_issue_id')
634:       end
635:     end
636: 
637:     if attrs['custom_field_values'].present?
638:       editable_custom_field_ids = editable_custom_field_values(user).map {|v| v.custom_field_id.to_s}
639:       attrs['custom_field_values'].select! {|k, v| editable_custom_field_ids.include?(k.to_s)}
640:     end
641: 
642:     if attrs['custom_fields'].present?
643:       editable_custom_field_ids = editable_custom_field_values(user).map {|v| v.custom_field_id.to_s}
644:       attrs['custom_fields'].select! {|c| editable_custom_field_ids.include?(c['id'].to_s)}
645:     end
646: 
647:     assign_attributes attrs
648:   end
649: 
650:   def disabled_core_fields
651:     tracker ? tracker.disabled_core_fields : []
652:   end

654:   # Returns the custom_field_values that can be edited by the given user
655:   def editable_custom_field_values(user=nil)
656:     read_only = read_only_attribute_names(user)
657:     visible_custom_field_values(user).reject do |value|
658:       read_only.include?(value.custom_field_id.to_s)
659:     end
660:   end
661: 
662:   # Returns the custom fields that can be edited by the given user
663:   def editable_custom_fields(user=nil)
664:     editable_custom_field_values(user).map(&:custom_field).uniq
665:   end
666: 
667:   # Returns the names of attributes that are read-only for user or the current user
668:   # For users with multiple roles, the read-only fields are the intersection of
669:   # read-only fields of each role
670:   # The result is an array of strings where sustom fields are represented with their ids
671:   #
672:   # Examples:
673:   #   issue.read_only_attribute_names # => ['due_date', '2']
674:   #   issue.read_only_attribute_names(user) # => []
675:   def read_only_attribute_names(user=nil)
676:     workflow_rule_by_attribute(user).reject {|attr, rule| rule != 'readonly'}.keys
677:   end
678: 
679:   # Returns the names of required attributes for user or the current user
680:   # For users with multiple roles, the required fields are the intersection of
681:   # required fields of each role
682:   # The result is an array of strings where sustom fields are represented with their ids
683:   #
684:   # Examples:
685:   #   issue.required_attribute_names # => ['due_date', '2']
686:   #   issue.required_attribute_names(user) # => []
687:   def required_attribute_names(user=nil)
688:     workflow_rule_by_attribute(user).reject {|attr, rule| rule != 'required'}.keys
689:   end
690: 
691:   # Returns true if the attribute is required for user
692:   def required_attribute?(name, user=nil)
693:     required_attribute_names(user).include?(name.to_s)
694:   end
695: 
696:   # Returns a hash of the workflow rule by attribute for the given user
697:   #
698:   # Examples:
699:   #   issue.workflow_rule_by_attribute # => {'due_date' => 'required', 'start_date' => 'readonly'}
700:   def workflow_rule_by_attribute(user=nil)
701:     return @workflow_rule_by_attribute if @workflow_rule_by_attribute && user.nil?
702: 
703:     roles = roles_for_workflow(user || User.current)
704:     return {} if roles.empty?
705: 
706:     result = {}
707:     workflow_permissions =
708:       WorkflowPermission.where(
709:         :tracker_id => tracker_id, :old_status_id => status_id,
710:         :role_id => roles.map(&:id)
711:       ).to_a
712:     if workflow_permissions.any?
713:       workflow_rules = workflow_permissions.inject({}) do |h, wp|
714:         h[wp.field_name] ||= {}
715:         h[wp.field_name][wp.role_id] = wp.rule
716:         h
717:       end
718:       fields_with_roles = {}
719:       IssueCustomField.where(:visible => false).
720:         joins(:roles).pluck(:id, "role_id").
721:           each do |field_id, role_id|
722:         fields_with_roles[field_id] ||= []
723:         fields_with_roles[field_id] << role_id
724:       end
725:       roles.each do |role|
726:         fields_with_roles.each do |field_id, role_ids|
727:           unless role_ids.include?(role.id)
728:             field_name = field_id.to_s
729:             workflow_rules[field_name] ||= {}
730:             workflow_rules[field_name][role.id] = 'readonly'
731:           end
732:         end
733:       end
734:       workflow_rules.each do |attr, rules|
735:         next if rules.size < roles.size
736: 
737:         uniq_rules = rules.values.uniq
738:         if uniq_rules.size == 1
739:           result[attr] = uniq_rules.first
740:         else
741:           result[attr] = 'required'
742:         end
743:       end
744:     end
745:     @workflow_rule_by_attribute = result if user.nil?
746:     result
747:   end
748:   private :workflow_rule_by_attribute

752:       status.default_done_ratio
753:     else
754:       read_attribute(:done_ratio)
755:     end
756:   end

766:   def validate_issue
767:     if due_date && start_date && (start_date_changed? || due_date_changed?) && due_date < start_date
768:       errors.add :due_date, :greater_than_start_date
769:     end
770: 
771:     if start_date && start_date_changed? && soonest_start && start_date < soonest_start
772:       errors.add :start_date, :earlier_than_minimum_start_date, :date => format_date(soonest_start)
773:     end
774: 
775:     if project && fixed_version_id
776:       if fixed_version.nil? || assignable_versions.exclude?(fixed_version)
777:         errors.add :fixed_version_id, :inclusion
778:       elsif reopening? && fixed_version.closed?
779:         errors.add :base, I18n.t(:error_can_not_reopen_issue_on_closed_version)
780:       end
781:     end
782: 
783:     if project && category_id
784:       unless project.issue_category_ids.include?(category_id)
785:         errors.add :category_id, :inclusion
786:       end
787:     end
788: 
789:     # Checks that the issue can not be added/moved to a disabled tracker
790:     if project && (tracker_id_changed? || project_id_changed?)
791:       if tracker && !project.trackers.include?(tracker)
792:         errors.add :tracker_id, :inclusion
793:       end
794:     end
795: 
796:     if project && assigned_to_id_changed? && assigned_to_id.present?
797:       unless assignable_users.include?(assigned_to)
798:         errors.add :assigned_to_id, :invalid
799:       end
800:     end
801: 
802:     # Checks parent issue assignment
803:     if @invalid_parent_issue_id.present?
804:       errors.add :parent_issue_id, :invalid
805:     elsif @parent_issue
806:       if !valid_parent_project?(@parent_issue)
807:         errors.add :parent_issue_id, :invalid
808:       elsif (@parent_issue != parent) && (
809:           self.would_reschedule?(@parent_issue) ||
810:           @parent_issue.self_and_ancestors.any? do |a|
811:             a.relations_from.any? do |r|
812:               r.relation_type == IssueRelation::TYPE_PRECEDES &&
813:                  r.issue_to.would_reschedule?(self)
814:             end
815:           end
816:         )
817:         errors.add :parent_issue_id, :invalid
818:       elsif !closed? && @parent_issue.closed?
819:         # cannot attach an open issue to a closed parent
820:         errors.add :base, :open_issue_with_closed_parent
821:       elsif !new_record?
822:         # moving an existing issue
823:         if move_possible?(@parent_issue)
824:           # move accepted
825:         else
826:           errors.add :parent_issue_id, :invalid
827:         end
828:       end
829:     end
830:   end

832:   # Validates the issue against additional workflow requirements
833:   def validate_required_fields
834:     user = new_record? ? author : current_journal.try(:user)
835: 
836:     required_attribute_names(user).each do |attribute|
837:       if /^\d+$/.match?(attribute)
838:         attribute = attribute.to_i
839:         v = custom_field_values.detect {|v| v.custom_field_id == attribute}
840:         if v && Array(v.value).detect(&:present?).nil?
841:           errors.add(v.custom_field.name, l('activerecord.errors.messages.blank'))
842:         end
843:       else
844:         if respond_to?(attribute) && send(attribute).blank? && !disabled_core_fields.include?(attribute)
845:           next if attribute == 'category_id' && project.try(:issue_categories).blank?
846:           next if attribute == 'fixed_version_id' && assignable_versions.blank?
847: 
848:           errors.add attribute, :blank
849:         end
850:       end
851:     end
852:   end

856:       unless allowed_target_trackers(@attributes_set_by).include?(tracker)
857:         errors.add :tracker, :invalid
858:       end
859:     end
860:   end

863:   # so that custom values that are not editable are not validated (eg. a custom field that
864:   # is marked as required should not trigger a validation error if the user is not allowed
865:   # to edit this field).

868:     if new_record? || custom_field_values_changed?
869:       editable_custom_field_values(user).each(&:validate_value)
870:     end

913:   # Returns a scope for journals that have an id greater than journal_id
914:   def journals_after(journal_id)
915:     scope = journals.reorder("#{Journal.table_name}.id ASC")
916:     if journal_id.present?
917:       scope = scope.where("#{Journal.table_name}.id > ?", journal_id.to_i)
918:     end
919:     scope
920:   end

922:   # Returns the journals that are visible to user with their index
923:   # Used to display the issue history
924:   def visible_journals_with_index(user=User.current)
925:     preloads = [:details, :updated_by]
926:     preloads << (Setting.gravatar_enabled? ? {user: :email_address} : :user)
927: 
928:     result = journals.
929:       preload(*preloads).
930:       reorder(:created_on, :id).
931:       to_a
932: 
933:     result.each_with_index {|j, i| j.indice = i + 1}
934: 
935:     unless user.allowed_to?(:view_private_notes, project)
936:       result.select! do |journal|
937:         !journal.private_notes? || journal.user == user
938:       end
939:     end
940:     Journal.preload_journals_details_custom_fields(result)
941:     result.select! {|journal| journal.notes? || journal.visible_details.any?}
942: 
943:     Journal.preload_reaction_details(result)
944: 
945:     result
946:   end

948:   # Returns the assignee immediately prior to the current one from the issue history
949:   def prior_assigned_to
950:     prior_assigned_to_id =
951:       journals.joins(:details)
952:               .where(details: {prop_key: 'assigned_to_id'})
953:               .where.not(details: {old_value: nil})
954:               .order(id: :desc)
955:               .pick(:old_value)
956: 
957:     prior_assigned_to_id && Principal.find_by(id: prior_assigned_to_id)
958:   end

962:   def status_was
963:     if status_id_changed?
964:       if status_id_was.to_i > 0
965:         @status_was ||= IssueStatus.find_by_id(status_id_was)
966:       end
967:     else
968:       @status_was ||= status
969:     end
970:   end

972:   # Return true if the issue is closed, otherwise false
973:   def closed?
974:     status.present? && status.is_closed?
975:   end

977:   # Returns true if the issue was closed when loaded
978:   def was_closed?
979:     status_was.present? && status_was.is_closed?
980:   end

982:   # Return true if the issue is being reopened
983:   def reopening?
984:     if new_record?
985:       false
986:     else
987:       status_id_changed? && !closed? && was_closed?
988:     end
989:   end

992:   # Return true if the issue is being closed
993:   def closing?
994:     if new_record?
995:       closed?
996:     else
997:       status_id_changed? && closed? && !was_closed?
998:     end
999:   end

1086:     initial_status = nil
1087:     if new_record?
1088:       # nop
1089:     elsif tracker_id_changed?
1090:       if Tracker.where(:id => tracker_id_was, :default_status_id => status_id_was).any?
1091:         initial_status = default_status
1092:       elsif tracker.issue_status_ids.include?(status_id_was)
1093:         initial_status = IssueStatus.find_by_id(status_id_was)
1094:       else
1095:         initial_status = default_status
1096:       end
1097:     else
1098:       initial_status = status_was
1099:     end
1100: 
1101:     initial_assigned_to_id = assigned_to_id_changed? ? assigned_to_id_was : assigned_to_id
1102:     assignee_transitions_allowed = initial_assigned_to_id.present? &&
1103:       (user.id == initial_assigned_to_id || user.group_ids.include?(initial_assigned_to_id))
1104: 
1105:     statuses = []
1106:     statuses += IssueStatus.new_statuses_allowed(
1107:       initial_status,
1108:       roles_for_workflow(user),
1109:       tracker,
1110:       author == user,
1111:       assignee_transitions_allowed
1112:     )
1113:     statuses << initial_status unless statuses.empty?
1114:     statuses << default_status if include_default || (new_record? && statuses.empty?)
1115: 
1116:     statuses = statuses.compact.uniq.sort
1117:     unless closable?
1118:       # cannot close a blocked issue or a parent with open subtasks
1119:       statuses.reject!(&:is_closed?)
1120:     end
1121:     unless reopenable?
1122:       # cannot reopen a subtask of a closed parent
1123:       statuses.select!(&:is_closed?)
1124:     end
1125:     statuses
1126:   end

1338:   # Returns true if this issue blocks the other issue, otherwise returns false
1339:   def blocks?(other)
1340:     all = [self]
1341:     last = [self]
1342:     while last.any?
1343:       current =
1344:         last.map do |i|
1345:           i.relations_from.where(:relation_type => IssueRelation::TYPE_BLOCKS).map(&:issue_to)
1346:         end.flatten.uniq
1347:       current -= last
1348:       current -= all
1349:       return true if current.include?(other)
1350: 
1351:       last = current
1352:       all += last
1353:     end
1354:     false
1355:   end

1358:   def would_reschedule?(other)
1359:     all = [self]
1360:     last = [self]
1361:     while last.any?
1362:       current = last.map do |i|
1363:         i.relations_from.where(:relation_type => IssueRelation::TYPE_PRECEDES).map(&:issue_to) +
1364:         i.leaves.to_a +
1365:         i.ancestors.map {|a| a.relations_from.where(:relation_type => IssueRelation::TYPE_PRECEDES).map(&:issue_to)}
1366:       end.flatten.uniq
1367:       current -= last
1368:       current -= all
1369:       return true if current.include?(other)
1370: 
1371:       last = current
1372:       all += last
1373:     end
1374:     false
1375:   end

1424:   # Reschedules the issue on the given date or the next working day and saves the record.
1425:   # If the issue is a parent task, this is done by rescheduling its subtasks.
1426:   def reschedule_on!(date, journal=nil)
1427:     return if date.nil?
1428: 
1429:     if leaf? || !dates_derived?
1430:       if start_date.nil? || start_date != date
1431:         if start_date && start_date > date
1432:           # Issue can not be moved earlier than its soonest start date
1433:           date = [soonest_start(true), date].compact.max
1434:         end
1435:         if journal
1436:           init_journal(journal.user)
1437:         end
1438:         reschedule_on(date)
1439:         begin
1440:           save
1441:         rescue ActiveRecord::StaleObjectError
1442:           reload
1443:           reschedule_on(date)
1444:           save
1445:         end
1446:       end
1447:     else
1448:       leaves.each do |leaf|
1449:         if leaf.start_date
1450:           # Only move subtask if it starts at the same date as the parent
1451:           # or if it starts before the given date
1452:           if start_date == leaf.start_date || date > leaf.start_date
1453:             leaf.reschedule_on!(date)
1454:           end
1455:         else
1456:           leaf.reschedule_on!(date)
1457:         end
1458:       end
1459:     end
1460:   end

1586:           "(#{Project.table_name}.lft >= :lft AND " \
1587:             "#{Project.table_name}.rgt <= :rgt) OR " \
1588:             "(#{Project.table_name}.lft < :lft AND #{Project.table_name}.rgt > :rgt)",
1589:           :lft => project.lft, :rgt => project.rgt
1590:         )
1591:     when 'descendants'
1592:       Issue.joins(:project).where("(#{Project.table_name}.lft >= :lft AND #{Project.table_name}.rgt <= :rgt)",
1593:                                   :lft => project.lft, :rgt => project.rgt)
1594:     else
1595:       Issue.where(:project_id => project.id)
1596:     end
1597:   end

1628:   # Query generator for selecting groups of issue counts for a project
1629:   # based on specific criteria
1630:   #
1631:   # Options
1632:   # * project - Project to search in.
1633:   # * with_subprojects - Includes subprojects issues if set to true.
1634:   # * association - Symbol. Association for grouping.
1635:   def self.count_and_group_by(options)
1636:     assoc = reflect_on_association(options[:association])
1637:     select_field = assoc.foreign_key
1638: 
1639:     Issue.
1640:       visible(User.current, :project => options[:project], :with_subprojects => options[:with_subprojects]).
1641:       joins(:status).
1642:       group(:status_id, :is_closed, select_field).
1643:       count.
1644:       map do |columns, total|
1645:         status_id, is_closed, field_value = columns
1646:         is_closed = ['t', 'true', '1'].include?(is_closed.to_s)
1647:         {
1648:           "status_id" => status_id.to_s,
1649:           "closed" => is_closed,
1650:           select_field => field_value.to_s,
1651:           "total" => total.to_s
1652:         }
1653:       end
1654:   end

1678:     condition = Project.allowed_to_condition(user, :add_issues)
1679:     if current_project

1681:     end

1695:   # Returns a scope of trackers that user can assign project issues to
1696:   def self.allowed_target_trackers(project, user=User.current, current_tracker=nil)
1697:     if project
1698:       scope = project.trackers.sorted
1699:       unless user.admin?
1700:         roles = user.roles_for_project(project).select {|r| r.has_permission?(:add_issues)}
1701:         unless roles.any? {|r| r.permissions_all_trackers?(:add_issues)}
1702:           tracker_ids = roles.map {|r| r.permissions_tracker_ids(:add_issues)}.flatten.uniq
1703:           if current_tracker
1704:             tracker_ids << current_tracker
1705:           end
1706:           scope = scope.where(:id => tracker_ids)
1707:         end
1708:       end
1709:       scope
1710:     else
1711:       Tracker.none
1712:     end
1713:   end

1717:   def user_tracker_permission?(user, permission)
1718:     if project && !project.active?
1719:       perm = Redmine::AccessControl.permission(permission)
1720:       return false unless perm && perm.read?
1721:     end
1722: 
1723:     if user.admin?
1724:       true
1725:     else
1726:       roles = user.roles_for_project(project).select {|r| r.has_permission?(permission)}
1727:       roles.any? do |r|
1728:         r.permissions_all_trackers?(permission) ||
1729:           r.permissions_tracker_ids?(permission, tracker_id)
1730:       end
1731:     end

1734:   def after_project_change
1735:     # Update project_id on related time entries
1736:     TimeEntry.where({:issue_id => id}).update_all(["project_id = ?", project_id])
1737: 
1738:     # Delete issue relations
1739:     unless Setting.cross_project_issue_relations?
1740:       relations_from.clear
1741:       relations_to.clear
1742:     end
1743: 
1744:     # Move subtasks that were in the same project
1745:     children.each do |child|
1746:       next unless child.project_id == project_id_before_last_save
1747: 
1748:       # Change project and keep project
1749:       child.send :project=, project, true
1750:       unless child.save
1751:         errors.add(
1752:           :base,
1753:           l(:error_move_of_child_not_possible,
1754:             :child => "##{child.id}",
1755:             :errors => child.errors.full_messages.join(", "))
1756:         )
1757:         raise ActiveRecord::Rollback
1758:       end
1759:     end
1760:   end

1762:   # Callback for after the creation of an issue by copy
1763:   # * adds a "copied to" relation with the copied issue
1764:   # * copies subtasks from the copied issue
1765:   def after_create_from_copy
1766:     return unless copy? && !@after_create_from_copy_handled
1767: 
1768:     if (@copied_from.project_id == project_id ||
1769:           Setting.cross_project_issue_relations?) &&
1770:         @copy_options[:link] != false
1771:       if @current_journal
1772:         @copied_from.init_journal(@current_journal.user)
1773:       end
1774:       relation =
1775:         IssueRelation.new(:issue_from => @copied_from, :issue_to => self,
1776:                           :relation_type => IssueRelation::TYPE_COPIED_TO)
1777:       unless relation.save
1778:         if logger
1779:           logger.error(
1780:             "Could not create relation while copying ##{@copied_from.id} to ##{id} " \
1781:               "due to validation errors: #{relation.errors.full_messages.join(', ')}"
1782:           )
1783:         end
1784:       end
1785:     end
1786: 
1787:     unless @copied_from.leaf? || @copy_options[:subtasks] == false
1788:       copy_options = (@copy_options || {}).merge(:subtasks => false)
1789:       copied_issue_ids = {@copied_from.id => self.id}
1790:       @copied_from.reload.descendants.reorder("#{Issue.table_name}.lft").each do |child|
1791:         # Do not copy self when copying an issue as a descendant of the copied issue
1792:         next if child == self
1793:         # Do not copy subtasks of issues that were not copied
1794:         next unless copied_issue_ids[child.parent_id]
1795: 
1796:         # Do not copy subtasks that are not visible to avoid potential disclosure of private data
1797:         unless child.visible?
1798:           if logger
1799:             logger.error(
1800:               "Subtask ##{child.id} was not copied during ##{@copied_from.id} copy " \
1801:                 "because it is not visible to the current user"
1802:             )
1803:           end
1804:           next
1805:         end
1806:         copy = Issue.new.copy_from(child, copy_options)
1807:         if @current_journal
1808:           copy.init_journal(@current_journal.user)
1809:         end
1810:         copy.author = author
1811:         copy.project = project
1812:         copy.parent_issue_id = copied_issue_ids[child.parent_id]
1813:         unless child.fixed_version.present? && child.fixed_version.status == 'open'
1814:           copy.fixed_version_id = nil
1815:         end
1816:         unless child.assigned_to_id.present? &&
1817:                  child.assigned_to.status == User::STATUS_ACTIVE
1818:           copy.assigned_to = nil
1819:         end
1820:         unless copy.save
1821:           if logger
1822:             logger.error(
1823:               "Could not copy subtask ##{child.id} " \
1824:                 "while copying ##{@copied_from.id} to ##{id} due to validation errors: " \
1825:                 "#{copy.errors.full_messages.join(', ')}"
1826:             )
1827:           end
1828:           next
1829:         end
1830:         copied_issue_ids[child.id] = copy.id
1831:       end
1832:     end
1833:     @after_create_from_copy_handled = true
1834:   end

1843:   # Updates the nested set for when an existing issue is moved
1844:   def update_nested_set_attributes_on_parent_change
1845:     former_parent_id = parent_id_before_last_save
1846:     # delete invalid relations of all descendants
1847:     self_and_descendants.each do |issue|
1848:       issue.relations.each do |relation|
1849:         relation.destroy unless relation.valid?
1850:       end
1851:     end
1852:     # update former parent
1853:     recalculate_attributes_for(former_parent_id) if former_parent_id
1854:   end

1859:       association(:parent).reset

1863:   def recalculate_attributes_for(issue_id)
1864:     if issue_id && p = Issue.find_by_id(issue_id)
1865:       if p.priority_derived?
1866:         # priority = highest priority of open children
1867:         # priority is left unchanged if all children are closed and there's no default priority defined
1868:         if priority_position =
1869:              p.children.open.joins(:priority).maximum("#{IssuePriority.table_name}.position")
1870:           p.priority = IssuePriority.find_by_position(priority_position)
1871:         elsif default_priority = IssuePriority.default
1872:           p.priority = default_priority
1873:         end
1874:       end
1875: 
1876:       if p.dates_derived?
1877:         # start/due dates = lowest/highest dates of children
1878:         p.start_date = p.children.minimum(:start_date)
1879:         p.due_date = p.children.maximum(:due_date)
1880:         if p.start_date && p.due_date && p.due_date < p.start_date
1881:           p.start_date, p.due_date = p.due_date, p.start_date
1882:         end
1883:       end
1884: 
1885:       if p.done_ratio_derived?
1886:         # done ratio = average ratio of children weighted with their total estimated hours
1887:         unless Issue.use_status_for_done_ratio? && p.status && p.status.default_done_ratio
1888:           children = p.children.to_a
1889:           if children.any?
1890:             child_with_total_estimated_hours = children.select {|c| c.total_estimated_hours.to_f > 0.0}
1891:             if child_with_total_estimated_hours.any?
1892:               average = Rational(
1893:                 child_with_total_estimated_hours.sum(&:total_estimated_hours).to_s,
1894:                 child_with_total_estimated_hours.count
1895:               )
1896:             else
1897:               average = Rational(1)
1898:             end
1899:             done = children.sum do |c|
1900:               estimated = Rational(c.total_estimated_hours.to_f.to_s)
1901:               estimated = average unless estimated > 0.0
1902:               ratio = c.closed? ? 100 : (c.done_ratio || 0)
1903:               estimated * ratio
1904:             end
1905:             progress = Rational(done, average * children.count)
1906:             p.done_ratio = progress.floor
1907:           end
1908:         end
1909:       end
1910: 
1911:       # ancestors will be recursively updated
1912:       p.save(:validate => false)
1913:     end
1914:   end

1917:   class << self
1918:     # Update issues so their versions are not pointing to a
1919:     # fixed_version that is not shared with the issue's project
1920:     def update_versions(conditions=nil)
1921:       # Only need to update issues with a fixed_version from
1922:       # a different project and that is not systemwide shared
1923:       Issue.joins(:project, :fixed_version).
1924:         where("#{Issue.table_name}.fixed_version_id IS NOT NULL" +
1925:           " AND #{Issue.table_name}.project_id <> #{::Version.table_name}.project_id" +
1926:           " AND #{::Version.table_name}.sharing <> 'system'").
1927:         where(conditions).each do |issue|
1928:         next if issue.project.nil? || issue.fixed_version.nil?
1929: 
1930:         unless issue.project.shared_versions.include?(issue.fixed_version)
1931:           retried = false
1932:           begin
1933:             issue.init_journal(User.current)
1934:             issue.fixed_version = nil
1935:             issue.save
1936:           rescue ActiveRecord::StaleObjectError
1937:             raise if retried
1938: 
1939:             retried = true
1940:             issue.reload
1941:             retry
1942:           end
1943:         end
1944:       end
1945:     end
1946:   end

2010:     if Setting.close_duplicate_issues? && closing?
2011:       duplicates.each do |duplicate|
2012:         # Reload is needed in case the duplicate was updated by a previous duplicate
2013:         duplicate.reload
2014:         # Don't re-close it if it's already closed
2015:         next if duplicate.closed?
2016: 
2017:         # Same user and notes
2018:         if @current_journal
2019:           duplicate.init_journal(@current_journal.user, @current_journal.notes)
2020:           duplicate.private_notes = @current_journal.private_notes

2022:         duplicate.update_attribute :status, self.status

2055:   def create_parent_issue_journal
2056:     return if persisted? && !saved_change_to_parent_id?
2057:     return if destroyed? && @without_nested_set_update
2058: 
2059:     child_id = self.id
2060:     old_parent_id, new_parent_id =
2061:       if persisted?
2062:         [parent_id_before_last_save, parent_id]
2063:       elsif destroyed?
2064:         [parent_id, nil]
2065:       else
2066:         [nil, parent_id]
2067:       end
2068: 
2069:     if old_parent_id.present?
2070:       Issue.transaction do
2071:         if old_parent_issue = Issue.visible.lock.find_by_id(old_parent_id)
2072:           old_parent_issue.init_journal(User.current)
2073:           old_parent_issue.current_journal.__send__(:add_attribute_detail, 'child_id', child_id, nil)
2074:           old_parent_issue.save
2075:         end
2076:       end
2077:     end
2078: 
2079:     if new_parent_id.present?
2080:       Issue.transaction do
2081:         if new_parent_issue = Issue.visible.lock.find_by_id(new_parent_id)
2082:           new_parent_issue.init_journal(User.current)
2083:           new_parent_issue.current_journal.__send__(:add_attribute_detail, 'child_id', nil, child_id)
2084:           new_parent_issue.save

2086:       end

20: class Journal < ApplicationRecord
21:   include Redmine::SafeAttributes
22:   include Redmine::Reaction::Reactable
23: 
24:   belongs_to :journalized, :polymorphic => true
25:   # added as a quick fix to allow eager loading of the polymorphic association
26:   # since always associated to an issue, for now
27:   belongs_to :issue, :foreign_key => :journalized_id
28: 
29:   belongs_to :user
30:   belongs_to :updated_by, :class_name => 'User'
31:   has_many :details, :class_name => "JournalDetail", :dependent => :delete_all, :inverse_of => :journal
32:   attr_accessor :indice
33: 
34:   acts_as_event(
35:     :title =>
36:        Proc.new do |o|
37:          status = ((s = o.new_status) ? " (#{s})" : nil)
38:          "#{o.issue.tracker} ##{o.issue.id}#{status}: #{o.issue.subject}"
39:        end,
40:     :description => :notes,
41:     :author => :user,
42:     :group => :issue,
43:     :type =>
44:       Proc.new do |o|
45:         (s = o.new_status) ? (s.is_closed? ? 'issue-closed' : 'issue-edit') : 'issue-note'
46:       end,
47:     :url =>
48:       Proc.new do |o|
49:         {:controller => 'issues', :action => 'show', :id => o.issue.id, :anchor => "change-#{o.id}"}
50:       end
51:   )
52:   acts_as_activity_provider(
53:     :type => 'issues',
54:     :author_key => :user_id,
55:     :scope =>
56:       proc do
57:         preload({:issue => :project}, {:issue => :tracker}, :user).
58:           joins("LEFT OUTER JOIN #{JournalDetail.table_name} ON #{JournalDetail.table_name}.journal_id = #{Journal.table_name}.id").
59:             where("#{Journal.table_name}.journalized_type = 'Issue' AND" +
60:                   " (#{JournalDetail.table_name}.prop_key = 'status_id' OR #{Journal.table_name}.notes <> '')").distinct
61:       end
62:   )
63:   acts_as_mentionable :attributes => ['notes']
64:   before_create :split_private_notes
65:   before_create :add_watcher
66:   after_create_commit :send_notification
67: 
68:   scope :visible, (lambda do |*args|
69:     user = args.shift || User.current
70:     options = args.shift || {}
71: 
72:     joins(:issue => :project).
73:       where(Issue.visible_condition(user, options)).
74:       where(Journal.visible_notes_condition(user, :skip_pre_condition => true))
75:   end)
76: 
77:   safe_attributes(
78:     'notes',
79:     :if => lambda {|journal, user| journal.new_record? || journal.editable_by?(user)})
80:   safe_attributes(
81:     'private_notes',
82:     :if => lambda {|journal, user| user.allowed_to?(:set_notes_private, journal.project)})
83:   safe_attributes 'updated_by'
84: 
85:   # Returns a SQL condition to filter out journals with notes that are not visible to user
86:   def self.visible_notes_condition(user=User.current, options={})
87:     private_notes_permission = Project.allowed_to_condition(user, :view_private_notes, options)
88:     sanitize_sql_for_conditions(["(#{table_name}.private_notes = ? OR #{table_name}.user_id = ? OR (#{private_notes_permission}))", false, user.id])
89:   end
90: 
91:   def initialize(*args)
92:     super
93:     if journalized
94:       if journalized.new_record?
95:         self.notify = false
96:       else
97:         start
98:       end
99:     end
100:   end
101: 
102:   def save(*args)
103:     journalize_changes
104:     # Do not save an empty journal
105:     notes_and_details_empty? ? false : super()
106:   end
107: 
108:   def notes_and_details_empty?
109:     notes.blank? && details.empty?
110:   end
111: 
112:   def journalized
113:     if journalized_type == 'Issue' && association(:issue).loaded?
114:       # Avoid extra query by using preloaded association
115:       issue
116:     else
117:       super
118:     end
119:   end
120: 
121:   # Returns journal details that are visible to user
122:   def visible_details(user=User.current)
123:     details.select do |detail|
124:       if detail.property == 'cf'
125:         detail.custom_field && detail.custom_field.visible_by?(project, user)
126:       elsif detail.property == 'relation'
127:         Issue.find_by_id(detail.value || detail.old_value).try(:visible?, user)
128:       else
129:         true
130:       end
131:     end
132:   end
133: 
134:   # Returns the JournalDetail for the given attribute, or nil if the attribute
135:   # was not updated
136:   def detail_for_attribute(attribute)
137:     details.detect {|detail| detail.prop_key == attribute}
138:   end
139: 
140:   # Returns the new status if the journal contains a status change, otherwise nil
141:   def new_status
142:     s = new_value_for('status_id')
143:     s ? IssueStatus.find_by_id(s.to_i) : nil
144:   end
145: 
146:   def new_value_for(prop)
147:     detail_for_attribute(prop).try(:value)
148:   end
149: 
150:   def editable_by?(usr)
151:     usr && usr.logged? && (usr.allowed_to?(:edit_issue_notes, project) || (self.user == usr && usr.allowed_to?(:edit_own_issue_notes, project)))
152:   end
153: 
154:   def project
155:     journalized.respond_to?(:project) ? journalized.project : nil
156:   end
157: 
158:   def attachments
159:     @attachments ||= begin
160:       ids = details.select {|d| d.property == 'attachment' && d.value.present?}.map(&:prop_key)
161:       ids.empty? ? [] : Attachment.where(id: ids).sort_by {|a| ids.index(a.id.to_s)}
162:     end
163:   end
164: 
165:   def visible?(*)
166:     journalized.visible?(*)
167:   end
168: 
169:   # Returns a string of css classes
170:   def css_classes
171:     s = +'journal'
172:     s << ' has-notes' unless notes.blank?
173:     s << ' has-details' unless details.blank?
174:     s << ' private-notes' if private_notes?
175:     s
176:   end
177: 
178:   def notify?
179:     @notify != false
180:   end
181: 
182:   def notify=(arg)
183:     @notify = arg
184:   end
185: 
186:   def notified_users
187:     notified = journalized.notified_users
188:     if private_notes?
189:       notified = notified.select {|user| user.allowed_to?(:view_private_notes, journalized.project)}
190:     end
191:     notified
192:   end
193: 
194:   def recipients
195:     notified_users.map(&:mail)
196:   end
197: 
198:   def notified_watchers
199:     notified = journalized.notified_watchers
200:     select_journal_visible_user(notified)
201:   end
202: 
203:   def notified_mentions
204:     notified = super
205:     select_journal_visible_user(notified)
206:   end
207: 
208:   def watcher_recipients
209:     notified_watchers.map(&:mail)
210:   end
211: 
212:   # Sets @custom_field instance variable on journals details using a single query
213:   def self.preload_journals_details_custom_fields(journals)
214:     field_ids = journals.map(&:details).flatten.select {|d| d.property == 'cf'}.map(&:prop_key).uniq
215:     if field_ids.any?
216:       fields_by_id = CustomField.where(:id => field_ids).index_by { |f| f.id }
217:       journals.each do |journal|
218:         journal.details.each do |detail|
219:           if detail.property == 'cf'
220:             detail.instance_variable_set :@custom_field, fields_by_id[detail.prop_key.to_i]
221:           end
222:         end
223:       end
224:     end
225:     journals
226:   end
227: 
228:   # Stores the values of the attributes and custom fields of the journalized object
229:   def start
230:     if journalized
231:       @attributes_before_change = journalized.journalized_attribute_names.index_with do |attribute|
232:         journalized.send(attribute)
233:       end
234:       @custom_values_before_change = journalized.custom_field_values.to_h do |c|
235:         [c.custom_field_id, c.value]
236:       end
237:     end
238:     self
239:   end
240: 
241:   # Adds a journal detail for an attachment that was added or removed
242:   def journalize_attachment(attachment, added_or_removed)
243:     key = (added_or_removed == :removed ? :old_value : :value)
244:     details <<
245:       JournalDetail.new(
246:         :property => 'attachment',
247:         :prop_key => attachment.id,
248:         key => attachment.filename
249:       )
250:   end
251: 
252:   # Adds a journal detail for an issue relation that was added or removed
253:   def journalize_relation(relation, added_or_removed)
254:     key = (added_or_removed == :removed ? :old_value : :value)
255:     details <<
256:       JournalDetail.new(
257:         :property  => 'relation',
258:         :prop_key  => relation.relation_type_for(journalized),
259:         key => relation.other_issue(journalized).try(:id)
260:       )
261:   end
262: 
263:   private
264: 
265:   # Generates journal details for attribute and custom field changes
266:   def journalize_changes
267:     # attributes changes
268:     if @attributes_before_change
269:       attrs = (journalized.journalized_attribute_names + @attributes_before_change.keys).uniq
270:       attrs.each do |attribute|
271:         before = @attributes_before_change[attribute]
272:         after = journalized.send(attribute)
273:         next if before == after || (before.blank? && after.blank?)
274: 
275:         add_attribute_detail(attribute, before, after)
276:       end
277:     end
278:     # custom fields changes
279:     if @custom_values_before_change
280:       values_by_custom_field_id = {}
281:       @custom_values_before_change.each_key do |custom_field_id|
282:         values_by_custom_field_id[custom_field_id] = nil
283:       end
284:       journalized.custom_field_values.each do |c|
285:         values_by_custom_field_id[c.custom_field_id] = c.value
286:       end
287: 
288:       values_by_custom_field_id.each do |custom_field_id, after|
289:         before = @custom_values_before_change[custom_field_id]
290:         next if before == after || (before.blank? && after.blank?)
291: 
292:         if before.is_a?(Array) || after.is_a?(Array)
293:           before = [before] unless before.is_a?(Array)
294:           after = [after] unless after.is_a?(Array)
295: 
296:           # values removed
297:           (before - after).reject(&:blank?).each do |value|
298:             add_custom_field_detail(custom_field_id, value, nil)
299:           end
300:           # values added
301:           (after - before).reject(&:blank?).each do |value|
302:             add_custom_field_detail(custom_field_id, nil, value)
303:           end
304:         else
305:           add_custom_field_detail(custom_field_id, before, after)
306:         end
307:       end
308:     end
309:     start
310:   end
311: 
312:   # Adds a journal detail for an attribute change
313:   def add_attribute_detail(attribute, old_value, value)
314:     add_detail('attr', attribute, old_value, value)
315:   end
316: 
317:   # Adds a journal detail for a custom field value change
318:   def add_custom_field_detail(custom_field_id, old_value, value)
319:     add_detail('cf', custom_field_id, old_value, value)
320:   end
321: 
322:   # Adds a journal detail
323:   def add_detail(property, prop_key, old_value, value)
324:     details <<
325:       JournalDetail.new(
326:         :property => property,
327:         :prop_key => prop_key,
328:         :old_value => old_value,
329:         :value => value
330:       )
331:   end
332: 
333:   def split_private_notes
334:     if private_notes?
335:       if notes.present?
336:         if details.any?
337:           # Split the journal (notes/changes) so we don't have half-private journals
338:           journal = Journal.new(:journalized => journalized, :user => user, :notes => nil, :private_notes => false)
339:           journal.details = details
340:           journal.save
341:           self.details = []
342:           self.created_on = journal.created_on
343:         end
344:       else
345:         # Blank notes should not be private
346:         self.private_notes = false
347:       end
348:     end
349:     true
350:   end
351: 
352:   def add_watcher
353:     if user.is_a?(User) &&
354:        user.pref.auto_watch_on?('issue_contributed_to') &&
355:        valid_watcher?(user)
356:       journalized.set_watcher(user, true)
357:     end
358: 
359:     assignee = journalized.assigned_to
360:     if assignee.is_a?(User) &&
361:        assignee.pref.auto_watch_on?('issue_assigned_to_me') &&
362:        valid_watcher?(assignee)
363:       journalized.set_watcher(assignee, true)
364:     end
365:   end
366: 
367:   def valid_watcher?(user)
368:     user.active? &&
369:       user.allowed_to?(:add_issue_watchers, journalized.project) &&
370:       journalized.valid_watcher?(user) &&
371:       !journalized.watched_by?(user)
372:   end
373: 
374:   def send_notification
375:     if notify? &&
376:         (
377:           Setting.notified_events.include?('issue_updated') ||
378:           (Setting.notified_events.include?('issue_note_added') && notes.present?) ||
379:           (Setting.notified_events.include?('issue_status_updated') && new_status.present?) ||
380:           (Setting.notified_events.include?('issue_assigned_to_updated') && detail_for_attribute('assigned_to_id').present?) ||
381:           (Setting.notified_events.include?('issue_priority_updated') && new_value_for('priority_id').present?) ||
382:           (Setting.notified_events.include?('issue_fixed_version_updated') && detail_for_attribute('fixed_version_id').present?) ||
383:           (Setting.notified_events.include?('issue_attachment_added') && details.any? {|d| d.property == 'attachment' && d.value })
384:         )
385:       Mailer.deliver_issue_edit(self)
386:     end
387:   end
388: 
389:   def select_journal_visible_user(notified)
390:     if private_notes?
391:       notified = notified.select {|user| user.allowed_to?(:view_private_notes, journalized.project)}
392:     end
393:     notified
394:   end
395: end

20: class IssueRelation < ApplicationRecord
21:   # Class used to represent the relations of an issue
22:   class Relations < Array
23:     include Redmine::I18n
24: 
25:     def initialize(issue, *)
26:       @issue = issue
27:       super(*)
28:     end
29: 
30:     def to_s(*args)
31:       map {|relation| relation.to_s(@issue)}.join(', ')
32:     end
33:   end
34: 
35:   include Redmine::SafeAttributes
36:   include Redmine::Utils::DateCalculation
37: 
38:   belongs_to :issue_from, :class_name => 'Issue'
39:   belongs_to :issue_to, :class_name => 'Issue'
40: 
41:   TYPE_RELATES      = "relates"
42:   TYPE_DUPLICATES   = "duplicates"
43:   TYPE_DUPLICATED   = "duplicated"
44:   TYPE_BLOCKS       = "blocks"
45:   TYPE_BLOCKED      = "blocked"
46:   TYPE_PRECEDES     = "precedes"
47:   TYPE_FOLLOWS      = "follows"
48:   TYPE_COPIED_TO    = "copied_to"
49:   TYPE_COPIED_FROM  = "copied_from"
50: 
51:   TYPES = {
52:     TYPE_RELATES =>     {:name => :label_relates_to, :sym_name => :label_relates_to,
53:                          :order => 1, :sym => TYPE_RELATES},
54:     TYPE_DUPLICATES =>  {:name => :label_duplicates, :sym_name => :label_duplicated_by,
55:                          :order => 2, :sym => TYPE_DUPLICATED},
56:     TYPE_DUPLICATED =>  {:name => :label_duplicated_by, :sym_name => :label_duplicates,
57:                          :order => 3, :sym => TYPE_DUPLICATES, :reverse => TYPE_DUPLICATES},
58:     TYPE_BLOCKS =>      {:name => :label_blocks, :sym_name => :label_blocked_by,
59:                          :order => 4, :sym => TYPE_BLOCKED},
60:     TYPE_BLOCKED =>     {:name => :label_blocked_by, :sym_name => :label_blocks,
61:                          :order => 5, :sym => TYPE_BLOCKS, :reverse => TYPE_BLOCKS},
62:     TYPE_PRECEDES =>    {:name => :label_precedes, :sym_name => :label_follows,
63:                          :order => 6, :sym => TYPE_FOLLOWS},
64:     TYPE_FOLLOWS =>     {:name => :label_follows, :sym_name => :label_precedes,
65:                          :order => 7, :sym => TYPE_PRECEDES, :reverse => TYPE_PRECEDES},
66:     TYPE_COPIED_TO =>   {:name => :label_copied_to, :sym_name => :label_copied_from,
67:                          :order => 8, :sym => TYPE_COPIED_FROM},
68:     TYPE_COPIED_FROM => {:name => :label_copied_from, :sym_name => :label_copied_to,
69:                          :order => 9, :sym => TYPE_COPIED_TO, :reverse => TYPE_COPIED_TO}
70:   }.freeze
71: 
72:   validates_presence_of :issue_from, :issue_to, :relation_type
73:   validates_inclusion_of :relation_type, :in => TYPES.keys
74:   validates_numericality_of :delay, :allow_nil => true
75:   validates_uniqueness_of :issue_to_id, :scope => :issue_from_id, :case_sensitive => true
76:   validate :validate_issue_relation
77: 
78:   before_save :handle_issue_order
79:   after_create  :call_issues_relation_added_callback
80:   after_destroy :call_issues_relation_removed_callback
81: 
82:   safe_attributes 'relation_type',
83:                   'delay',
84:                   'issue_to_id'
85: 
86:   def safe_attributes=(attrs, user=User.current)
87:     if attrs.respond_to?(:to_unsafe_hash)
88:       attrs = attrs.to_unsafe_hash
89:     end
90:     return unless attrs.is_a?(Hash)
91: 
92:     attrs = attrs.deep_dup
93:     if issue_id = attrs.delete('issue_to_id')
94:       if issue_id.to_s.strip =~ /\A#?(\d+)\z/
95:         issue_id = $1.to_i
96:         self.issue_to = Issue.visible(user).find_by_id(issue_id)
97:       end
98:     end
99: 
100:     super(attrs)
101:   end
102: 
103:   def visible?(user=User.current)
104:     (issue_from.nil? || issue_from.visible?(user)) && (issue_to.nil? || issue_to.visible?(user))
105:   end
106: 
107:   def deletable?(user=User.current)
108:     visible?(user) &&
109:       ((issue_from.nil? || user.allowed_to?(:manage_issue_relations, issue_from.project)) ||
110:         (issue_to.nil? || user.allowed_to?(:manage_issue_relations, issue_to.project)))
111:   end
112: 
113:   def initialize(attributes=nil, *args)
114:     super
115:     if new_record?
116:       if relation_type.blank?
117:         self.relation_type = IssueRelation::TYPE_RELATES
118:       end
119:     end
120:   end
121: 
122:   def validate_issue_relation
123:     if issue_from && issue_to
124:       errors.add :issue_to_id, :invalid if issue_from_id == issue_to_id
125:       unless issue_from.project_id == issue_to.project_id ||
126:                 Setting.cross_project_issue_relations?
127:         errors.add :issue_to_id, :not_same_project
128:       end
129:       if circular_dependency?
130:         errors.add :base, :circular_dependency
131:       end
132:       if issue_from.is_descendant_of?(issue_to) || issue_from.is_ancestor_of?(issue_to)
133:         errors.add :base, :cant_link_an_issue_with_a_descendant
134:       end
135:     end
136:   end
137: 
138:   def other_issue(issue)
139:     (self.issue_from_id == issue.id) ? issue_to : issue_from
140:   end
141: 
142:   # Returns the relation type for +issue+
143:   def relation_type_for(issue)
144:     if TYPES[relation_type]
145:       if self.issue_from_id == issue.id
146:         relation_type
147:       else
148:         TYPES[relation_type][:sym]
149:       end
150:     end
151:   end
152: 
153:   def label_for(issue)
154:     if TYPES[relation_type]
155:       TYPES[relation_type][(self.issue_from_id == issue.id) ? :name : :sym_name]
156:     else
157:       :unknow
158:     end
159:   end
160: 
161:   def to_s(issue=nil)
162:     issue ||= issue_from
163:     issue_text = block_given? ? yield(other_issue(issue)) : "##{other_issue(issue).try(:id)}"
164:     s = []
165:     s << l(label_for(issue))
166:     s << "(#{l('datetime.distance_in_words.x_days', :count => delay)})" if delay && delay != 0
167:     s << issue_text
168:     s.join(' ')
169:   end
170: 
171:   def css_classes_for(issue)
172:     "rel-#{relation_type_for(issue)}"
173:   end
174: 
175:   def handle_issue_order
176:     reverse_if_needed
177: 
178:     if relation_type == TYPE_PRECEDES
179:       self.delay ||= 0
180:     else
181:       self.delay = nil
182:     end
183:     set_issue_to_dates
184:   end
185: 
186:   def set_issue_to_dates(journal=nil)
187:     soonest_start = self.successor_soonest_start
188:     if soonest_start && issue_to
189:       issue_to.reschedule_on!(soonest_start, journal)
190:     end
191:   end
192: 
193:   def successor_soonest_start
194:     if (self.relation_type == TYPE_PRECEDES) && delay && issue_from &&
195:            (issue_from.start_date || issue_from.due_date)
196:       add_working_days((issue_from.due_date || issue_from.start_date), (1 + delay))
197:     end
198:   end
199: 
200:   def <=>(relation)
201:     return nil unless relation.is_a?(IssueRelation)
202: 
203:     r = TYPES[self.relation_type][:order] <=> TYPES[relation.relation_type][:order]
204:     r == 0 ? id <=> relation.id : r
205:   end
206: 
207:   def init_journals(user)
208:     issue_from.init_journal(user) if issue_from
209:     issue_to.init_journal(user) if issue_to
210:   end
211: 
212:   private
213: 
214:   # Reverses the relation if needed so that it gets stored in the proper way
215:   # Should not be reversed before validation so that it can be displayed back
216:   # as entered on new relation form.
217:   #
218:   # Orders relates relations by ID, so that uniqueness index in DB is triggered
219:   # on concurrent access.
220:   def reverse_if_needed
221:     if TYPES.has_key?(relation_type) && TYPES[relation_type][:reverse]
222:       issue_tmp = issue_to
223:       self.issue_to = issue_from
224:       self.issue_from = issue_tmp
225:       self.relation_type = TYPES[relation_type][:reverse]
226: 
227:     elsif relation_type == TYPE_RELATES && issue_from_id > issue_to_id
228:       self.issue_to, self.issue_from = issue_from, issue_to
229:     end
230:   end
231: 
232:   # Returns true if the relation would create a circular dependency
233:   def circular_dependency?
234:     case relation_type
235:     when 'follows'
236:       issue_from.would_reschedule? issue_to
237:     when 'precedes'
238:       issue_to.would_reschedule? issue_from
239:     when 'blocked'
240:       issue_from.blocks? issue_to
241:     when 'blocks'
242:       issue_to.blocks? issue_from
243:     when 'relates'
244:       self.class.where(issue_from_id: issue_to, issue_to_id: issue_from).present?
245:     else
246:       false
247:     end
248:   end
249: 
250:   def call_issues_relation_added_callback
251:     call_issues_callback :relation_added
252:   end
253: 
254:   def call_issues_relation_removed_callback
255:     call_issues_callback :relation_removed
256:   end
257: 
258:   def call_issues_callback(name)
259:     [issue_from, issue_to].each do |issue|
260:       if issue
261:         issue.send name, self
262:       end
263:     end
264:   end
265: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: # Redmine - project management software

19: 
20: class TimeEntry < ApplicationRecord
21:   include Redmine::SafeAttributes
22:   # could have used polymorphic association
23:   # project association here allows easy loading of time entries at project level with one database trip
24:   belongs_to :project
25:   belongs_to :issue
26:   belongs_to :user
27:   belongs_to :author, :class_name => 'User'
28:   belongs_to :activity, :class_name => 'TimeEntryActivity'
29: 
30:   acts_as_customizable
31:   acts_as_event(
32:     :title =>
33:       Proc.new do |o|
34:         related   = o.issue if o.issue && o.issue.visible?
35:         related ||= o.project
36:         "#{l_hours(o.hours)} (#{related.event_title})"
37:       end,
38:     :url =>
39:       Proc.new do |o|
40:         {:controller => 'timelog', :action => 'index', :project_id => o.project, :issue_id => o.issue}
41:       end,
42:     :author => :user,
43:     :group => :issue,
44:     :description => :comments
45:   )
46:   acts_as_activity_provider :timestamp => "#{table_name}.created_on",
47:                             :author_key => :user_id,
48:                             :scope => proc {joins(:project).preload(:project)}
49:   acts_as_webhookable
50: 
51:   validates_presence_of :author_id, :user_id, :activity_id, :project_id, :hours, :spent_on
52:   validates_presence_of :issue_id, :if => lambda {Setting.timelog_required_fields.include?('issue_id')}
53:   validates_presence_of :comments, :if => lambda {Setting.timelog_required_fields.include?('comments')}
54:   validates_numericality_of :hours, :allow_nil => true, :message => :invalid
55:   validates_length_of :comments, :maximum => 1024, :allow_nil => true
56:   validates :spent_on, :date => true
57:   before_validation :set_project_if_nil
58:   # TODO: remove this, author should be always explicitly set
59:   before_validation :set_author_if_nil
60:   validate :validate_time_entry
61: 
62:   scope :visible, (lambda do |*args|
63:     joins(:project).
64:     where(TimeEntry.visible_condition(args.shift || User.current, *args))
65:   end)
66:   scope :left_join_issue, (lambda do
67:     joins(
68:       "LEFT OUTER JOIN #{Issue.table_name}" \
69:       " ON #{Issue.table_name}.id = #{TimeEntry.table_name}.issue_id" \
70:       " AND (#{Issue.visible_condition(User.current)})"
71:     )
72:   end)
73:   scope :on_issue, (lambda do |issue|
74:     joins(:issue).
75:     where("#{Issue.table_name}.root_id = #{issue.root_id} AND #{Issue.table_name}.lft >= #{issue.lft} AND #{Issue.table_name}.rgt <= #{issue.rgt}")
76:   end)
77: 
78:   safe_attributes 'user_id', 'hours', 'comments', 'project_id',
79:                   'issue_id', 'activity_id', 'spent_on',
80:                   'custom_field_values', 'custom_fields'
81: 
82:   def webhook_payload_api_template
83:     "app/views/timelog/show.api.rsb"
84:   end
85: 
86:   # Returns a SQL conditions string used to find all time entries visible by the specified user
87:   def self.visible_condition(user, options={})
88:     Project.allowed_to_condition(user, :view_time_entries, options) do |role, user|
89:       if role.time_entries_visibility == 'all'
90:         nil
91:       elsif role.time_entries_visibility == 'own' && user.id && user.logged?
92:         "#{table_name}.user_id = #{user.id}"
93:       else
94:         '1=0'
95:       end
96:     end
97:   end
98: 
99:   # Returns true if user or current user is allowed to view the time entry
100:   def visible?(user=nil)
101:     (user || User.current).allowed_to?(:view_time_entries, self.project) do |role, user|
102:       if role.time_entries_visibility == 'all'
103:         true
104:       elsif role.time_entries_visibility == 'own'
105:         self.user == user
106:       else
107:         false
108:       end
109:     end
110:   end
111: 
112:   def initialize(attributes=nil, *args)
113:     super
114:     if new_record? && self.activity.nil?
115:       self.activity_id = TimeEntryActivity.default_activity_id(User.current, self.project)
116:       self.hours = nil if hours == 0
117:     end
118:   end
119: 
120:   def safe_attributes=(attrs, user=User.current)
121:     if attrs
122:       attrs = super(attrs)
123:       if issue_id_changed? && issue
124:         if issue.visible?(user) && user.allowed_to?(:log_time, issue.project)
125:           if attrs[:project_id].blank? && issue.project_id != project_id
126:             self.project_id = issue.project_id
127:           end
128:           @invalid_issue_id = nil
129:         elsif user.allowed_to?(:log_time, issue.project) && issue.assigned_to_id_changed? && issue.previous_assignee == User.current
130:           current_assignee = issue.assigned_to
131:           issue.assigned_to = issue.previous_assignee
132:           unless issue.visible?(user)
133:             @invalid_issue_id = issue_id
134:           end
135:           issue.assigned_to = current_assignee
136:         else
137:           @invalid_issue_id = issue_id
138:         end
139:       end
140:       if user_id_changed? && user_id != author_id && !user.allowed_to?(:log_time_for_other_users, project)
141:         @invalid_user_id = user_id
142:       else
143:         @invalid_user_id = nil
144:       end
145: 
146:       # Delete assigned custom fields not visible by the user
147:       editable_custom_field_ids = editable_custom_field_values(user).map {|v| v.custom_field_id.to_s}
148:       self.custom_field_values.delete_if do |c|
149:         !editable_custom_field_ids.include?(c.custom_field.id.to_s)
150:       end
151:     end
152: 
153:     attrs
154:   end
155: 
156:   def set_project_if_nil
157:     self.project = issue.project if issue && project.nil?
158:   end
159: 
160:   def set_author_if_nil
161:     self.author = User.current if author.nil?
162:   end
163: 
164:   def validate_time_entry
165:     if hours
166:       errors.add :hours, :invalid if hours < 0
167:       errors.add :hours, :invalid if hours == 0.0 && hours_changed? && !Setting.timelog_accept_0_hours?
168: 
169:       max_hours = Setting.timelog_max_hours_per_day.to_f
170:       if hours_changed? && max_hours > 0.0
171:         logged_hours = other_hours_with_same_user_and_day
172:         if logged_hours + hours > max_hours
173:           errors.add(
174:             :base,
175:             I18n.t(:error_exceeds_maximum_hours_per_day,
176:                    :logged_hours => format_hours(logged_hours),
177:                    :max_hours => format_hours(max_hours)))
178:         end
179:       end
180:     end
181:     errors.add :project_id, :invalid if project.nil?
182:     if @invalid_user_id || (user_id_changed? && user_id != author_id && !self.assignable_users.map(&:id).include?(user_id))
183:       errors.add :user_id, :invalid
184:     end
185:     errors.add :issue_id, :invalid if (issue_id && !issue) || (issue && project!=issue.project) || @invalid_issue_id
186:     errors.add :activity_id, :inclusion if activity_id_changed? && project && !project.activities.include?(activity)
187:     if spent_on && spent_on_changed? && user
188:       errors.add :base, I18n.t(:error_spent_on_future_date) if !Setting.timelog_accept_future_dates? && (spent_on > user.today)
189:     end
190:     if !Setting.timelog_accept_closed_issues? && issue&.closed? && issue.was_closed?
191:       errors.add :base, I18n.t(:error_spent_on_closed_issue)
192:     end
193:   end
194: 
195:   def hours=(h)
196:     write_attribute :hours, (h.is_a?(String) ? (h.to_hours || h) : h)
197:   end
198: 
199:   def hours
200:     h = read_attribute(:hours)
201:     if h.is_a?(Float)
202:       # Convert the float value to a rational with a denominator of 60 to
203:       # avoid floating point errors.
204:       #
205:       # Examples:
206:       #  0.38333333333333336 => (23/60)   # 23m
207:       #  0.9913888888888889  => (59/60)   # 59m 29s is rounded to 59m
208:       #  0.9919444444444444  => (1/1)     # 59m 30s is rounded to 60m
209:       (h * 60).round / 60r
210:     else
211:       h
212:     end
213:   end
214: 
215:   # tyear, tmonth, tweek assigned where setting spent_on attributes
216:   # these attributes make time aggregations easier
217:   def spent_on=(date)
218:     super
219:     self.tyear = spent_on ? spent_on.year : nil
220:     self.tmonth = spent_on ? spent_on.month : nil
221:     self.tweek = spent_on ? Date.civil(spent_on.year, spent_on.month, spent_on.day).cweek : nil
222:   end
223: 
224:   # Returns true if the time entry can be edited by usr, otherwise false
225:   def editable_by?(usr)
226:     visible?(usr) && (
227:       (usr == user && usr.allowed_to?(:edit_own_time_entries, project)) || usr.allowed_to?(:edit_time_entries, project)
228:     )
229:   end
230: 
231:   # Returns the custom_field_values that can be edited by the given user
232:   def editable_custom_field_values(user=nil)
233:     visible_custom_field_values(user)
234:   end
235: 
236:   # Returns the custom fields that can be edited by the given user
237:   def editable_custom_fields(user=nil)
238:     editable_custom_field_values(user).map(&:custom_field).uniq
239:   end
240: 
241:   def visible_custom_field_values(user = nil)
242:     user ||= User.current
243:     custom_field_values.select do |value|
244:       value.custom_field.visible_by?(project, user)
245:     end
246:   end
247: 
248:   def assignable_users
249:     users = []
250:     if project
251:       user_ids =
252:         project.members.active.preload(:roles).filter_map do |m|
253:           m.roles.any? {|role| role.allowed_to?(:log_time)} ? m.user_id : nil
254:         end.uniq
255:       users = User.where(:id => user_ids).sorted.to_a
256:     end
257:     users << User.current if User.current.logged? && !users.include?(User.current)
258:     users
259:   end
260: 
261:   private
262: 
263:   # Returns the hours that were logged in other time entries for the same user and the same day
264:   def other_hours_with_same_user_and_day
265:     if user_id && spent_on
266:       TimeEntry.
267:         where(:user_id => user_id, :spent_on => spent_on).
268:         where.not(:id => id).
269:         sum(:hours).to_f
270:     else
271:       0.0
272:     end
273:   end
274: end

121:   def file=(incoming_file)
122:     unless incoming_file.nil?
123:       @temp_file = incoming_file
124:       if @temp_file.respond_to?(:original_filename)
125:         self.filename = @temp_file.original_filename
126:         self.filename.force_encoding("UTF-8")
127:       end

144:   # Copies the temporary file to its final location
145:   # and computes its hash
146:   def files_to_final_location
147:     if @temp_file
148:       self.disk_directory = target_directory
149:       sha = Digest::SHA256.new
150:       Attachment.create_diskfile(filename, disk_directory) do |f|
151:         self.disk_filename = File.basename f.path
152:         logger.info("Saving attachment '#{self.diskfile}' (#{@temp_file.size} bytes)") if logger
153:         if @temp_file.respond_to?(:read)
154:           buffer = ""
155:           while (buffer = @temp_file.read(8192))
156:             f.write(buffer)
157:             sha.update(buffer)
158:           end
159:         else
160:           f.write(@temp_file)
161:           sha.update(@temp_file)
162:         end
163:       end
164:       self.digest = sha.hexdigest
165:     end
166:     @temp_file = nil
167: 
168:     if content_type.blank? && filename.present?
169:       self.content_type = Redmine::MimeType.of(filename)
170:     end
171:     # Don't save the content type if it's longer than the authorized length
172:     if self.content_type && self.content_type.length > 255
173:       self.content_type = nil
174:     end
175:   end

180:       delete_from_disk!

239:   # Returns the full path the attachment thumbnail, or nil
240:   # if the thumbnail cannot be generated.
241:   def thumbnail(options={})
242:     if thumbnailable? && readable?
243:       size = options[:size].to_i
244:       if size > 0
245:         # Limit the number of thumbnails per image
246:         size = (size / 50.0).ceil * 50
247:         # Maximum thumbnail size
248:         size = 800 if size > 800
249:       else
250:         size = Setting.thumbnails_size.to_i
251:       end
252:       size = 100 unless size > 0
253:       target = thumbnail_path(size)
254: 
255:       begin
256:         # TODO: Stop passing the deprecated is_pdf flag in Redmine 7.0
257:         Redmine::Thumbnail.generate(self.diskfile, target, size, is_pdf?)
258:       rescue => e
259:         if logger
260:           logger.error(
261:             "An error occured while generating thumbnail for #{disk_filename} " \
262:               "to #{target}\nException was: #{e.message}"
263:           )
264:         end
265:         nil
266:       end
267:     end
268:   end

283:   def is_text?
284:     Redmine::MimeType.is_type?('text', filename) || Redmine::SyntaxHighlighting.filename_supported?(filename)
285:   end

292:     Redmine::MimeType.of(filename) == 'text/x-textile'

319:   def markdownized_preview_content
320:     return nil unless markdownized_previewable?
321: 
322:     target = markdownized_preview_cache_path
323:     if Redmine::Markdownizer.convert(diskfile, target)
324:       File.read(target, :mode => "rb")

329:         "An error occured while generating markdownized preview for #{disk_filename} " \
330:           "to #{target}\nException was: #{e.message}"
331:       )
332:     end

349:   # Returns the attachment token

351:     "#{id}.#{digest}"

354:   # Finds an attachment that matches the given token and that has no container
355:   def self.find_by_token(token)
356:     if token.to_s =~ /^(\d+)\.([0-9a-f]+)$/
357:       attachment_id, attachment_digest = $1, $2
358:       attachment = Attachment.find_by(:id => attachment_id, :digest => attachment_digest)
359:       if attachment && attachment.container.nil?
360:         attachment
361:       end
362:     end
363:   end

365:   # Bulk attaches a set of files to an object
366:   #
367:   # Returns a Hash of the results:
368:   # :files => array of the attached files
369:   # :unsaved => array of the files that could not be attached
370:   def self.attach_files(obj, attachments)
371:     result = obj.save_attachments(attachments, User.current)
372:     obj.attach_saved_attachments
373:     result
374:   end
375: 
376:   # Updates the filename and description of a set of attachments
377:   # with the given hash of attributes. Returns true if all
378:   # attachments were updated.
379:   #
380:   # Example:
381:   #   Attachment.update_attachments(attachments, {
382:   #     4 => {:filename => 'foo'},
383:   #     7 => {:filename => 'bar', :description => 'file description'}
384:   #   })
385:   #
386:   def self.update_attachments(attachments, params)
387:     converted = {}
388:     params.each {|key, val| converted[key.to_i] = val}
389:     saved = true
390:     transaction do
391:       attachments.each do |attachment|
392:         if file = converted[attachment.id]
393:           attachment.filename = file[:filename] if file.key?(:filename)
394:           attachment.description = file[:description] if file.key?(:description)
395:           saved &&= attachment.save
396:         end
397:       end
398:       unless saved
399:         raise ActiveRecord::Rollback
400:       end
401:     end
402:     saved
403:   end
