405:   def self.latest_attach(attachments, filename)
406:     return unless filename.valid_encoding?
407: 
408:     attachments.sort_by{|attachment| [attachment.created_on, attachment.id]}.reverse.detect do |att|
409:       filename.casecmp?(att.filename)
410:     end
411:   end
412: 
413:   def self.prune(age=1.day)
414:     Attachment.where("created_on < ? AND (container_type IS NULL OR container_type = '')", Time.now - age).destroy_all
415:   end
416: 
417:   def self.archive_attachments(attachments)
418:     attachments = attachments.select(&:readable?)
419:     return nil if attachments.blank?
420: 
421:     Zip.unicode_names = true
422:     archived_file_names = []
423:     buffer = Zip::OutputStream.write_buffer do |zos|
424:       attachments.each do |attachment|
425:         filename = attachment.filename
426:         # rename the file if a file with the same name already exists
427:         dup_count = 0
428:         while archived_file_names.include?(filename)
429:           dup_count += 1
430:           extname = File.extname(attachment.filename)
431:           basename = File.basename(attachment.filename, extname)
432:           filename = "#{basename}(#{dup_count})#{extname}"
433:         end
434:         zos.put_next_entry(filename)
435:         zos << IO.binread(attachment.diskfile)
436:         archived_file_names << filename
437:       end
438:     end
439:     buffer.string
440:   ensure
441:     buffer&.close
442:   end
443: 
444:   # Moves an existing attachment to its target directory
445:   def move_to_target_directory!
446:     return unless !new_record? & readable?
447: 
448:     src = diskfile
449:     self.disk_directory = target_directory
450:     dest = diskfile
451: 
452:     return if src == dest
453: 
454:     unless FileUtils.mkdir_p(File.dirname(dest))
455:       logger.error "Could not create directory #{File.dirname(dest)}" if logger
456:       return
457:     end
458: 
459:     unless FileUtils.mv(src, dest)
460:       logger.error "Could not move attachment from #{src} to #{dest}" if logger
461:       return
462:     end
463: 
464:     update_column :disk_directory, disk_directory
465:   end

467:   # Moves existing attachments that are stored at the root of the files
468:   # directory (ie. created before Redmine 2.3) to their target subdirectories
469:   def self.move_from_root_to_target_directory
470:     Attachment.where("disk_directory IS NULL OR disk_directory = ''").find_each do |attachment|
471:       attachment.move_to_target_directory!
472:     end
473:   end
474: 
475:   # Updates digests to SHA256 for all attachments that have a MD5 digest
476:   # (ie. created before Redmine 3.4)
477:   def self.update_digests_to_sha256
478:     Attachment.where("length(digest) < 64").find_each do |attachment|
479:       attachment.update_digest_to_sha256!
480:     end
481:   end
482: 
483:   # Updates attachment digest to SHA256
484:   def update_digest_to_sha256!
485:     if readable?
486:       sha = Digest::SHA256.new
487:       File.open(diskfile, 'rb') do |f|
488:         while buffer = f.read(8192)
489:           sha.update(buffer)
490:         end
491:       end
492:       update_column :digest, sha.hexdigest
493:     end
494:   end
495: 
496:   # Returns true if the extension is allowed regarding allowed/denied
497:   # extensions defined in application settings, otherwise false
498:   def self.valid_extension?(extension)
499:     denied, allowed = [:attachment_extensions_denied, :attachment_extensions_allowed].map do |setting|
500:       Setting.send(setting)
501:     end
502:     if denied.present? && extension_in?(extension, denied)
503:       return false
504:     end
505:     if allowed.present? && !extension_in?(extension, allowed)
506:       return false
507:     end
508: 
509:     true
510:   end
511: 
512:   # Returns true if extension belongs to extensions list.
513:   def self.extension_in?(extension, extensions)
514:     extension = extension.downcase.sub(/\A\.+/, '')
515: 
516:     unless extensions.is_a?(Array)
517:       extensions = extensions.to_s.split(",").map(&:strip)
518:     end
519:     extensions = extensions.map {|s| s.downcase.sub(/\A\.+/, '')}.reject(&:blank?)
520:     extensions.include?(extension)
521:   end
522: 
523:   # Returns true if attachment's extension belongs to extensions list.
524:   def extension_in?(extensions)
525:     self.class.extension_in?(File.extname(filename), extensions)
526:   end
527: 
528:   # returns either MD5 or SHA256 depending on the way self.digest was computed
529:   def digest_type
530:     digest.size < 64 ? "MD5" : "SHA256" if digest.present?
531:   end

533:   private
534: 
535:   def reuse_existing_file_if_possible
536:     original_diskfile = diskfile
537:     original_filename = disk_filename
538:     reused = with_lock do
539:       if existing = Attachment
540:                       .where(digest: self.digest, filesize: self.filesize)
541:                       .where.not(disk_filename: original_filename)
542:                       .order(:id)
543:                       .last
544:         existing.with_lock do
545:           existing_diskfile = existing.diskfile
546:           if File.readable?(original_diskfile) &&
547:             File.readable?(existing_diskfile) &&
548:             FileUtils.identical?(original_diskfile, existing_diskfile)
549:             self.update_columns disk_directory: existing.disk_directory,
550:                                 disk_filename: existing.disk_filename
551:           end
552:         end
553:       end
554:     end
555:     if reused && Attachment.where(disk_filename: original_filename).none?
556:       File.delete(original_diskfile)
557:     end
558:   rescue ActiveRecord::StatementInvalid, ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound
559:     # Catch and ignore lock errors. It is not critical if deduplication does
560:     # not happen, therefore we do not retry.
561:     # with_lock throws ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound if the record isnt there
562:     # anymore, thats why this is caught and ignored as well.
563:   end
564: 
565:   # Physically deletes the file from the file system
566:   def delete_from_disk!
567:     FileUtils.rm_f(diskfile) if disk_filename.present?
568:     Dir[thumbnail_path("*")].each do |thumb|
569:       File.delete(thumb)
570:     end
571:     FileUtils.rm_f(markdownized_preview_cache_path)
572:   end
573: 
574:   def thumbnail_path(size)
575:     File.join(self.class.thumbnails_storage_path,
576:               "#{digest}_#{filesize}_#{size}.thumb")
577:   end
578: 
579:   def sanitize_filename(value)
580:     # get only the filename, not the whole path
581:     just_filename = value.gsub(/\A.*(\\|\/)/m, '')
582: 
583:     # Finally, replace invalid characters with underscore
584:     just_filename.gsub(/[\/?%*:|"'<>\n\r]+/, '_')
585:   end
586: 
587:   # Returns the subdirectory in which the attachment will be saved
588:   def target_directory
589:     time = created_on || DateTime.now
590:     time.strftime("%Y/%m")
591:   end
592: 
593:   # Singleton class method is public
594:   class << self
595:     # Claims a unique ASCII or hashed filename, yields the open file handle
596:     def create_diskfile(filename, directory=nil, &)
597:       timestamp = DateTime.now.strftime("%y%m%d%H%M%S")
598:       ascii = ''
599:       if %r{^[a-zA-Z0-9_.-]*$}.match?(filename) && filename.length <= 50
600:         ascii = filename
601:       else
602:         ascii = ActiveSupport::Digest.hexdigest(filename)
603:         # keep the extension if any
604:         ascii << $1 if filename =~ %r{(\.[a-zA-Z0-9]+)$}
605:       end
606: 
607:       path = File.join storage_path, directory.to_s
608:       FileUtils.mkdir_p(path) unless File.directory?(path)
609:       begin
610:         name = "#{timestamp}_#{ascii}"
611:         File.open(
612:           File.join(path, name),
613:           flags: File::CREAT | File::EXCL | File::WRONLY,
614:           binmode: true,
615:           &
616:         )
617:       rescue Errno::EEXIST
618:         timestamp.succ!
619:         retry
620:       end
621:     end
622:   end
623: end

596:   # Sends reminders to issue assignees
597:   # Available options:
598:   # * :days     => how many days in the future to remind about (defaults to 7)
599:   # * :tracker  => id of tracker for filtering issues (defaults to all trackers)
600:   # * :project  => id or identifier of project to process (defaults to all projects)
601:   # * :users    => array of user/group ids who should be reminded
602:   # * :version  => name of target version for filtering issues (defaults to none)
603:   def self.reminders(options={})
604:     days = options[:days] || 7
605:     project = options[:project] ? Project.find(options[:project]) : nil
606:     tracker = options[:tracker] ? Tracker.find(options[:tracker]) : nil
607:     target_version_id = options[:version] ? Version.named(options[:version]).pluck(:id) : nil
608:     if options[:version] && target_version_id.blank?
609:       raise ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound.new("Couldn't find Version named #{options[:version]}")
610:     end
611: 
612:     user_ids = options[:users]
613: 
614:     scope =
615:       Issue.open.where(
616:         "#{Issue.table_name}.assigned_to_id IS NOT NULL" \
617:           " AND #{Project.table_name}.status = #{Project::STATUS_ACTIVE}" \
618:           " AND #{Issue.table_name}.due_date <= ?", days.day.from_now.to_date
619:       )
620:     scope = scope.where(:assigned_to_id => user_ids) if user_ids.present?
621:     scope = scope.where(:project_id => project.id) if project
622:     scope = scope.where(:fixed_version_id => target_version_id) if target_version_id.present?
623:     scope = scope.where(:tracker_id => tracker.id) if tracker
624:     issues_by_assignee = scope.includes(:status, :assigned_to, :project, :tracker).
625:                               group_by(&:assigned_to)
626:     issues_by_assignee.keys.each do |assignee|  # rubocop:disable Style/HashEachMethods
627:       if assignee.is_a?(Group)
628:         assignee.users.each do |user|
629:           issues_by_assignee[user] ||= []
630:           issues_by_assignee[user] += issues_by_assignee[assignee]
631:         end
632:       end
633:     end
634: 
635:     issues_by_assignee.each do |assignee, issues|
636:       if assignee.is_a?(User) && assignee.active? && issues.present?
637:         visible_issues = issues.select {|i| i.visible?(assignee)}
638:         visible_issues.sort!{|a, b| (a.due_date <=> b.due_date).nonzero? || (a.id <=> b.id)}
639:         reminder(assignee, visible_issues, days).deliver_later if visible_issues.present?
640:       end
641:     end
642:   end

656:   # Rake will likely end, causing the in-process thread pool to be deleted, before

659:     adapter = ActionMailer::MailDeliveryJob.queue_adapter

217:       # all issues can be referenced/fixed
218:     elsif issue

220:       unless issue.project &&

237:   # Updates the +issue+ according to +action+
238:   def fix_issue(issue, action)
239:     # the issue may have been updated by the closure of another one (eg. duplicate)

242:     return if issue.closed?
243: 
244:     journal = issue.init_journal(user || User.anonymous,

248:     rule = Setting.commit_update_keywords_array.detect do |rule|
249:       rule['keywords'].include?(action) &&
250:         (rule['if_tracker_id'].blank? || rule['if_tracker_id'] == issue.tracker_id.to_s)

22:     case in_or_out
23:     when :in
24:       if gantt.zoom < 4
25:         link_to(
26:           sprite_icon('zoom-in', l(:text_zoom_in)),
27:           {:params => request.query_parameters.merge(gantt.params.merge(:zoom => (gantt.zoom + 1)))},
28:           :class => 'icon icon-zoom-in')
29:       else
30:         content_tag(:span, sprite_icon('zoom-in', l(:text_zoom_in)), :class => 'icon icon-zoom-in').html_safe
31:       end
32: 
33:     when :out
34:       if gantt.zoom > 1
35:         link_to(
36:           sprite_icon('zoom-out', l(:text_zoom_out)),
37:           {:params => request.query_parameters.merge(gantt.params.merge(:zoom => (gantt.zoom - 1)))},
38:           :class => 'icon icon-zoom-out')
39:       else
40:         content_tag(:span, sprite_icon('zoom-out', l(:text_zoom_out)), :class => 'icon icon-zoom-out').html_safe
41:       end
42:     end
43:   end
44: 
45:   def gantt_chart_tag(query, &)
46:     data_attributes = {
47:       controller: 'gantt--chart',
48:       # Events emitted by child controllers the chart listens to.
49:       # - `gantt--options` toggles checkboxes under Options.
50:       # - `gantt--subjects` reports tree expand/collapse.
51:       # - Window resize triggers a redraw of progress lines and relations.
52:       action: %w(
53:         gantt--options:toggle-display@document->gantt--chart#handleOptionsDisplay
54:         gantt--options:toggle-relations@document->gantt--chart#handleOptionsRelations
55:         gantt--options:toggle-progress@document->gantt--chart#handleOptionsProgress
56:         gantt--subjects:toggle-tree->gantt--chart#handleSubjectTreeChanged
57:         resize@window->gantt--chart#handleWindowResize
58:       ).join(' '),
59:       'gantt--chart-issue-relation-types-value': Redmine::Helpers::Gantt::DRAW_TYPES.to_json,
60:       'gantt--chart-show-selected-columns-value': query.draw_selected_columns ? 'true' : 'false',
61:       'gantt--chart-show-relations-value': query.draw_relations ? 'true' : 'false',
62:       'gantt--chart-show-progress-value': query.draw_progress_line ? 'true' : 'false'
63:     }
64: 
65:     tag.table(class: 'gantt-table', data: data_attributes, &)
66:   end
67: 
68:   def gantt_column_tag(column_name, min_width: nil, **options, &)
69:     options[:data] = {
70:       controller: 'gantt--column',
71:       action: 'resize@window->gantt--column#handleWindowResize',
72:       'gantt--column-min-width-value': min_width,
73:       'gantt--column-column-value': column_name
74:     }
75:     options[:class] = ["gantt_#{column_name}_column", options[:class]]
76: 
77:     tag.td(**options, &)
78:   end
79: 
80:   def gantt_subjects_tag(&)
81:     data_attributes = {
82:       controller: 'gantt--subjects',
83:       action: 'gantt--column:resize-column-subjects@document->gantt--subjects#handleResizeColumn'
84:     }
85:     tag.div(class: "gantt_subjects", data: data_attributes, &)
86:   end
87: end

51:       Rails.logger.warn("Unknown block \"#{block}\" found in #{user.login} (id=#{user.id}) preferences")
52:       return
53:     end
54: 
55:     settings = user.pref.my_page_settings(block)
56:     if partial = block_definition[:partial]
57:       begin
58:         render(:partial => partial, :locals => {:user => user, :settings => settings, :block => block})
59:       rescue ActionView::MissingTemplate
60:         Rails.logger.warn("Partial \"#{partial}\" missing for block \"#{block}\" found in #{user.login} (id=#{user.id}) preferences")

62:       end

264:   # Helper that formats object for html or text rendering
265:   # Options:
266:   # * :html - If true, format the object as HTML (default: true)
267:   # * :thousands_delimiter - If true, format the numeric object with thousands delimiter (default: false)
268:   def format_object(object, *args, &block)
269:     options =
270:       if args.first.is_a?(Hash)
271:         args.first
272:       elsif !args.empty?
273:         # Support the old syntax `format_object(object, html_flag)`
274:         # TODO: Display a deprecation warning in a future version, then remove this
275:         {:html => args.first}
276:       else
277:         {}
278:       end
279: 
280:     html = options.fetch(:html, true)
281:     thousands_delimiter = options.fetch(:thousands_delimiter, false)
282:     delimiter_char = thousands_delimiter ? ::I18n.t('number.format.delimiter') : nil
283: 
284:     if block
285:       object = yield object
286:     end
287:     case object
288:     when Array
289:       formatted_objects = object.map do |o|
290:         format_object(o, html: html, thousands_delimiter: thousands_delimiter)
291:       end
292:       html ? safe_join(formatted_objects, ', ') : formatted_objects.join(', ')
293:     when Time, ActiveSupport::TimeWithZone
294:       format_time(object)
295:     when Date
296:       format_date(object)
297:     when Integer
298:       number_with_delimiter(object, delimiter: delimiter_char)
299:     when Float
300:       number_with_delimiter(sprintf('%.2f', object), delimiter: delimiter_char)
301:     when User, Group
302:       html ? link_to_principal(object) : object.to_s
303:     when Project
304:       html ? link_to_project(object) : object.to_s
305:     when Version
306:       html ? link_to_version(object) : object.to_s
307:     when TrueClass
308:       l(:general_text_Yes)
309:     when FalseClass
310:       l(:general_text_No)
311:     when Issue
312:       object.visible? && html ? link_to_issue(object) : "##{object.id}"
313:     when Attachment
314:       if html
315:         content_tag(
316:           :span,
317:           link_to_attachment(object) +
318:           link_to_attachment(
319:             object,
320:             :class => ['icon-only', 'icon-download'],
321:             :icon => 'download',
322:             :title => l(:button_download),
323:             :download => true
324:           ),
325:           class: 'attachment-filename'
326:         )
327:       else
328:         object.filename
329:       end
330:     when CustomValue, CustomFieldValue
331:       return "" unless object.customized&.visible?
332: 
333:       if object.custom_field
334:         f = object.custom_field.format.formatted_custom_value(self, object, html)
335:         if f.nil? || f.is_a?(String)
336:           f
337:         else
338:           format_object(f, html: html, thousands_delimiter: object.custom_field.thousands_delimiter?, &block)
339:         end
340:       else
341:         object.value.to_s
342:       end
343:     else
344:       html ? h(object) : object.to_s
345:     end
346:   end

1257:             if is_custom_filter
1258:               sql =

1260:                   "CAST(CASE #{db_table}.#{db_field} WHEN '' THEN '0' " \

1262:             else
1263:               sql = "#{db_table}.#{db_field} IN (#{int_values})"
1264:             end

20: class SearchController < ApplicationController
21:   before_action :find_optional_project_by_id, :authorize_global
22:   accept_api_auth :index
23: 
24:   def index
25:     @question = params[:q]&.strip || ""
26:     @all_words = params[:all_words] ? params[:all_words].present? : true
27:     @titles_only = params[:titles_only] ? params[:titles_only].present? : false
28:     @search_attachments = params[:attachments].presence || '0'
29:     @open_issues = params[:open_issues] ? params[:open_issues].present? : false
30: 
31:     case params[:format]
32:     when 'xml', 'json'
33:       @offset, @limit = api_offset_and_limit
34:     else
35:       @offset = nil
36:       @limit = Setting.search_results_per_page.to_i
37:       @limit = 10 if @limit == 0
38:     end
39: 
40:     # quick jump to an issue
41:     if !api_request? && (m = @question.match(/^#?(\d+)$/)) && (issue = Issue.visible.find_by_id(m[1].to_i))
42:       redirect_to issue_path(issue)
43:       return
44:     end
45: 
46:     projects_to_search =
47:       case params[:scope]
48:       when 'all'
49:         nil
50:       when 'my_projects'
51:         User.current.projects
52:       when 'bookmarks'
53:         Project.where(id: User.current.bookmarked_project_ids)
54:       when 'subprojects'
55:         @project ? (@project.self_and_descendants.to_a) : nil
56:       else
57:         @project
58:       end
59: 
60:     @object_types = Redmine::Search.available_search_types.dup
61:     if projects_to_search.is_a? Project
62:       # don't search projects
63:       @object_types.delete('projects')
64:       # only show what the user is allowed to view
65:       @object_types = @object_types.select {|o| User.current.allowed_to?(:"view_#{o}", projects_to_search)}
66:     end
67: 
68:     @scope = @object_types.select {|t| params[t].present?}
69:     @scope = @object_types if @scope.empty?
70: 
71:     fetcher = Redmine::Search::Fetcher.new(
72:       @question, User.current, @scope, projects_to_search,
73:       :all_words => @all_words, :titles_only => @titles_only, :attachments => @search_attachments, :open_issues => @open_issues,
74:       :cache => params[:page].present?, :params => params.to_unsafe_hash
75:     )
76: 
77:     if fetcher.tokens.present?
78:       @result_count = fetcher.result_count
79:       @result_count_by_type = fetcher.result_count_by_type
80:       @tokens = fetcher.tokens
81: 
82:       @result_pages = Paginator.new @result_count, @limit, params['page']
83:       @offset ||= @result_pages.offset
84:       @results = fetcher.results(@offset, @result_pages.per_page)
85:     else
86:       @question = ""
87:     end
88:     respond_to do |format|
89:       format.html {render :layout => false if request.xhr?}
90:       format.api do
91:         @results ||= []
92:         render :layout => false
93:       end
94:     end
95:   end
96: end

277:       @diff = @repository.diff(@path, @rev, @rev_to)
278:       (show_error_not_found; return) unless @diff

297:         (show_error_not_found; return) unless @diff
